comments.reader: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in reading. She is developing her reading skills steadily and has made good progress with her reading fluency this year. This is an important skill that will unlock deeper comprehension as texts become more complex. Her retrieval skills are developing well and she is becoming more confident in finding and recording information from texts. Building her reading vocabulary through daily reading of quality texts will support both her comprehension and her writing. Reading widely and regularly in Year 5 will be central to Asel's continued progress across all areas of her learning.
comments.writer: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in writing. She approaches her writing with care and has made steady progress across the year, particularly in her spelling of key year group patterns and her handwriting fluency. Asel writes clearly in both fiction and non-fiction genres and is developing her ability to organise her ideas into paragraphs. The main areas for Asel to focus on are her composition skills – developing more ambitious vocabulary choices and building stronger connections between her sentences and ideas. Her punctuation and grammar accuracy, particularly with fronted adverbials and inverted commas for direct speech, will be an important area to strengthen in Year 5. Drawing more actively on her reading for vocabulary and sentence structures will help Asel show more of what she is capable of as a writer.
comments.mathematician: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in mathematics. The key areas for Asel to develop in Year 5 are her mathematical reasoning and problem-solving skills – building confidence to explain her thinking and tackle unfamiliar problems with different approaches. Her strong times table fluency gives her an excellent foundation for the more complex mathematical challenges ahead.
